dental claims to payers.</p>
<ol>
<li>Call this endpoint with a JSON payload.</li>
<li>Stedi translates your request to the X12 837 EDI format and sends it to the payer.</li>
<li>The endpoint returns a response from Stedi in JSON format containing information about the claim you submitted and whether the submission was successful.</li>
</ol><div class="flex items-end justify-between gap-2"><h2 class="flex scroll-m-28 flex-row items-center gap-2 group api-section-heading-title" depth="2" slug="authorizations" id="authorizations"><a empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A unique string to identify this request to the server. The key can be up to 255 characters. You can safely retry requests with the same idempotency key within 24 hours of making the first request. This prevents you from sending duplicate claims due to network errors or other intermittent failures. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/api-reference/index#idempotency-keys" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a>.</p></div></div></div><nav class="api-section-nav"><div class="flex gap-4 items-center"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the assistant surgeon who rendered the care. Use this object when the rendering providers provided these services in the role of the assistant surgeon.</p>
<p>This should be an individual, not an organization, and you should supply at least the surgeon&#x27;s <code>lastName</code>, <code>taxonomyCode</code>, and an identifier, which is typically the <code>npi</code>.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The phone number, formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB is the telephone number prefix, and CCCC is the telephone number. The phone number should only include the digits 0 to 9. Don&#x27;t include separators, such as dashes, and don&#x27;t include long distance access numbers, such as 1. For example, you would format the phone number 123-456-7890 as 1234567890.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code from the National Uniform Claims Committee <a href="https://taxonomy.nucc.org/" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set</a>. This identifies the provider&#x27;s type and/or area of specialty. For example, code <code>	1223S0112X</code> is for Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" 13.0142 18.5759 12.2628 19.3738 12C18.5759 11.7372 18 10.9858 18 10.1V6C18 5.44772 17.5523 5 17 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the billing provider.</p>
<ul>
<li>For tax identification, you <strong>must</strong> include either the provider&#x27;s Social Security Number (SSN) in the <code>ssn</code> property <em>or</em> their Employer Identification Number (EIN) in the <code>employerId</code> property, but not both.</li>
<li>If the billing provider has an NPI, you must include it in the <code>npi</code> property. If the billing provider does not have an NPI, you must include either the <code>commercialNumber</code> or the <code>locationNumber</code> for identification. Some payers may require the <code>npi</code> <strong>and</strong> either the <code>commercialNumber</code> or the <code>locationNumber</code> as a secondary identifier.</li>
<li>Some solo providers may use their SSN as their EIN. In this case, submit the SSN in the <code>ssn</code> property and leave the <code>employerId</code> property blank.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.billing" id="body.billing"><summary class="inline-flex items-center rounded-md p-2 font-medium transition-colors duration-100 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-fd-ring border hover:bg-fd-accent hover:text-fd-accent-foreground gap-1 text-xs px-3 py-2 cursor-pointer list-none justify-start empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The billing provider&#x27;s address. This must be a physical practice location where care is delivered. If the billing provider receives mail at a PO Box, lockbox, or other non-physical address, you can provide that address in the <code>payToAddress</code> object.</p>
<p>For United States addresses, you <strong>must</strong> include the full nine-digit zip code with no separators, such as <code>100031502</code>. If you don&#x27;t know the full zip code, you can find it using the <a href="https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">USPS ZIP Code Lookup</a> tool.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The phone number, formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB is the telephone number prefix, and CCCC is the telephone number. The phone number should only include the digits 0 to 9. Don&#x27;t include separators, such as dashes, and don&#x27;t include long distance access numbers, such as 1. For example, you would format the phone number 123-456-7890 as 1234567890.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The billing provider&#x27;s Employer Identification Number (EIN). Typically a string of exactly nine numbers with no separators, unless otherwise instructed by the payer. If you include this value, you cannot include the <code>ssn</code>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none billing provider&#x27;s Social Security Number. Must be a string of exactly nine numbers with no separators. If you include this value, you cannot include the <code>employerId</code>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none This is assigned directly by a payer in order to identify the provider in their system. This is not commonly used.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code from the National Uniform Claims Committee <a href="https://taxonomy.nucc.org/" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Health Care Provider Taxonomy Code Set</a>. This identifies the billing provider&#x27;s type and/or area of specialty.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A code specifying the type of transaction. Defaults to <code>CH</code> if not provided.</p>
<ul>
<li><code>31</code>: Only for use by state Medicaid agencies performing post payment recovery.</li>
<li><code>CH</code>: Use when the transaction contains only fee for service claims or claims with at least one chargeable line item. Also use when it&#x27;s not clear whether a transaction contains claims or capitated encounters, or if the transaction contains a mix of claims and capitated encounters.</li>
<li><code>RP</code>: Use for capitated encounters. Also use when the transaction is being sent to an entity for purposes other than adjudication of a claim. For example, when you&#x27;re sending the claim to a state health agency that is using the claim for health data reporting purposes.</li>
</ul><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>31</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>CH</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>RP</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the healthcare claim.</p>
<p>Note that the objects and properties marked as required are required for all claims, while others are conditionally required, depending on type of claim and claim circumstances. For example, you must always provide the <code>placeOfServiceCode</code> property, but you only need to provide the <code>otherSubscriberInformation</code> object in coordination of benefits scenarios. When you include a conditionally required object, you must provide all of its required properties.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A code indicating whether the patient or an authorized person has authorized the plan to remit payment directly to the provider. Use <code>W</code> when the patient refuses to assign benefits. Can be set to <code>N</code> - No (Payment should go to the patient), <code>Y</code> - Yes (Payment should go directly to the provider), or <code>W</code> - Not Applicable (use when patient refuses to assign benefits).</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>N</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>W</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>Y</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Dates related to services within the claim, including the date an appliance was placed and the date of the accident (if applicable). All dates apply to all services in the claim unless specifically overridden within an individual service line.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The date of the accident related to this claim. Required when <code>relatedCausesCode</code> is set to <code>AA</code> - Auto Accident or <code>OA</code> - Other Accident. Also required when <code>relatedCausesCode</code> is set to <code>EM</code> - Employment and this claim is the result of an accident.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A code identifying the type of claim. For example <code>DS</code> - Disability.</p>
<ul>
<li>Use <code>OF</code> when submitting Medicare Part D claims.</li>
<li>Use <code>ZZ</code> when you don&#x27;t know the type of insurance.</li>
<li>Some payers reject claims with invalid codes. If you&#x27;re not sure which code to use, we recommend running a <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-eligibility" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">real-time eligibility check</a> and using the value returned in the most relevant <code>benefitsInformation.insuranceTypeCode</code> property. Note that the eligibility response uses a different code list than claims, so you may need to map that code value to the appropriate claim filing code.</li>
</ul>
<p>Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/claims-code-lists#claim-filing-indicator-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Claims code lists</a> for a complete text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p><a href="https://www.nubc.org/system/files/media/file/2019/06/billTypeFrequencyCodes837.pdf" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Bill Type Frequency Code</a> specifying the frequency of the claim. Can be set to <code>1</code> - Admit thru Discharge Claim, <code>7</code> - Replacement of Prior Claim, or <code>8</code> - Void/Cancel of Prior Claim.</p>
<ul>
<li>Set this to <code>1</code> when you&#x27;re submitting a new claim and when you&#x27;re resubmitting a claim that was rejected before it entered the payer&#x27;s processing system. You must also set this property to <code>1</code> for every resubmission to Original Medicare because Original Medicare doesn&#x27;t accept code <code>7</code>.</li>
<li>Set this to <code>7</code> when you need to resubmit a corrected claim that the payer has already processed. These are claims that the payer has already adjudicated or claims that the payer has rejected with a 277CA containing the Payer Claim Control Number (PCCN), indicating it has entered the payer&#x27;s system.</li>
<li>When resubmitting with code <code>7</code> or voiding with code <code>8</code>, you must also include the Payer Claim Control Number (sometimes called the ICN) in the <code>claimInformation.claimSupplementalInformation.claimControlNumber</code> property. An exception is Original Medicare, which requires that you omit the Payer Claim Control Number from resubmissions.</li>
<li>For resubmissions and cancellations, we strongly recommend including a unique Patient Control Number in the <code>claimInformation.patientControlNumber</code> for tracking purposes.</li>
</ul>
<p>Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/resubmit-cancel-claims" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Resubmit or cancel claims</a> for complete details.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>1</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>7</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>8</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 Providers should not complete this property.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>This is the Payer Claim Control Number (PCCN) for an existing claim that this claim is meant to replace or cancel. This property is generally <strong>required</strong> when the <code>claimInformation.claimFrequencyCode</code> is set to <code>7</code> or <code>8</code>. One exception to this guidance is Original Medicare, which specifies that you omit the PCCN from resubmissions.</p>
<p>Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/resubmit-cancel-claims" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Resubmit or cancel claims</a> for complete details and information about where to find the PCCN for an existing claim.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible text-[13px] bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-950/50 text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-deprecated-pill">Deprecated</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The claim number assigned by clearinghouse, van, etc.</p>
<p>Stedi overwrites this value when it sends the claim to the payer, so you shouldn&#x27;t include this property in your request. We strongly recommend using the <code>claimInformation.patientControlNumber</code> property as your claim tracking ID.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The Predetermination of Benefits Identification Number assigned by the payer. Required for services that have been previously predetermined and are now being submitted for payment. The identifier you supply here applies to the entire claim.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Required when an authorization number is assigned by the payer or UMO <em>and</em> the services on this claim were preauthorized. This authorization number applies to the payer you listed in the <code>receiver</code> object. If you need to include authorization numbers for other payers, you can include them in <code>claimInformation.otherSubscriberInformation.otherPayerName.otherPayerPriorAuthorizationNumber</code>.</p>
<p>Generally, preauthorization numbers are assigned by the payer or UMO to authorize a service prior to its being performed. The UMO (Utilization Management Organization) is generally the entity empowered to make a decision regarding the outcome of a health services review or the owner of information.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 is assigned by the payer or Utilization Management Organization (UMO) <em>and</em> a referral is involved.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x when you plan to submit an <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/submit-claim-attachments" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">attachment</a> for the claim electronically through Stedi APIs or SFTP, when there is a paper attachment following this claim, or when the provider deems it necessary to identify additional information that is being held at the provider&#x27;s office and is available upon request.</p>
<p>Use this object when there is a single attachment for the claim. If there are multiple attachments, use the <code>reportInformations</code> array instead.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A control number assigned to the attachment. The payer uses this identifier to match the attachment to the claim.</p>
<ul>
<li>You must include either this property or <code>attachmentId</code> in the request, but not both. Including both properties will result in an error.</li>
<li>We recommend using a ULID or UUID of up to 50 characters.</li>
<li>Stedi autogenerates a control number if you don&#x27;t provide one.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Regex pattern:<!-- --> <code class="font-mono">^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$</code></span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Length:<!-- --> <!-- -->36</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The unique identifier for the attachment file you previously uploaded to Stedi. This value is returned in the <code>attachmentId</code> property of the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-submit-claim-attachment" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Create Claim Attachment (275) JSON</a> response. Stedi uses it to generate and submit the 275 claim attachment transaction to the payer.</p>
<ul>
<li>This property is <strong>required</strong> when you&#x27;re submitting attachment files through Stedi.</li>
<li>You must include either this property or <code>attachmentControlNumber</code> in the request, but not both. Including both properties will result in an error.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating the title or contents of a document, report or supporting item. For example, <code>B4</code> - Referral Form or <code>DA</code> - Dental Models. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/claims-code-lists#attachment-report-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Claims code lists</a> for a complete list.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code identifying the method by which the provider&#x27;s report is attached. Can be set to <code>AA</code> - Available on Request at Provider Site, <code>BM</code> - By Mail, <code>EL</code> - Electronically Only, <code>EM</code> - E-Mail, <code>FT</code> - File Transfer, or <code>FX</code> - By Fax.</p>
<p>Set this to <code>EL</code> when you plan to submit attachments electronically through Stedi APIs.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>An array of report information for the claim. Use this when you need to submit multiple report information records. You can submit up to 10 objects in this array.</p>
<p>Required when you plan to submit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/submit-claim-attachments" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">attachments</a> for the claim electronically through Stedi APIs or SFTP, when there is a paper attachment following this claim, or when the provider deems it necessary to identify that they have additional information at their office that is available upon request.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A control number assigned to the attachment. The payer uses this identifier to match the attachment to the claim.</p>
<ul>
<li>You must include either this property or <code>attachmentId</code> in the request, but not both. Including both properties will result in an error.</li>
<li>We recommend using a ULID or UUID of up to 50 characters.</li>
<li>Stedi autogenerates a control number if you don&#x27;t provide one.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Regex pattern:<!-- --> <code class="font-mono">^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$</code></span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Length:<!-- --> <!-- -->36</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The unique identifier for the attachment file you previously uploaded to Stedi. This value is returned in the <code>attachmentId</code> property of the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-submit-claim-attachment" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Create Claim Attachment (275) JSON</a> response. Stedi uses it to generate and submit the 275 claim attachment transaction to the payer.</p>
<ul>
<li>This property is <strong>required</strong> when you&#x27;re submitting attachment files through Stedi.</li>
<li>You must include either this property or <code>attachmentControlNumber</code> in the request, but not both. Including both properties will result in an error.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none For example, <code>B4</code> - Referral Form or <code>DA</code> - Dental Models. provider&#x27;s report is attached. Can be set to <code>AA</code> - Available on Request at Provider Site, <code>BM</code> - By Mail, <code>EL</code> - Electronically Only, <code>EM</code> - E-Mail, <code>FT</code> - File Transfer, or <code>FX</code> - By Fax.</p>
<p>Set this to <code>EL</code> when you plan to submit attachments electronically through Stedi APIs.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Items:<!-- --> <!-- -->1 - 4</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Details about the patient&#x27;s healthcare diagnosis. Only required when the diagnosis may have an impact on the adjudication of the claim in cases where specific dental procedures may minimize the risks associated with the connection between the patient&#x27;s oral and systemic health conditions.</p>
<ul>
<li>Use <code>ABK</code> as the type for the principal diagnosis code and <code>ABF</code> for any other diagnosis codes you include.</li>
<li>Use one <code>ABK</code> code as the first object, and then you can submit up to 3 <code>ABF</code> codes as needed. If you need to submit more codes than this, you must create additional, separate claims.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The diagnosis code.</p>
<ul>
<li>You must submit a valid, billable code at the highest level of specificity. Include the 4th - 7th characters as applicable.</li>
<li><strong>Don&#x27;t</strong> submit the decimal point for ICD codes. The decimal point is implied.</li>
<li><strong>Don&#x27;t</strong> submit ICD-10 header codes. Header codes exist to group related codes and aren&#x27;t valid for billing. These header codes can change with each new version of ICD-10, so we recommend reviewing your diagnosis codes every year to ensure that they aren&#x27;t classified as header codes in the most recent version. To determine whether a code is a header code, you can also search the <a href="https://vsac.nlm.nih.gov/context/cs" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Value Set Authority Center</a>. If the &#x27;Header&#x27; property is set, the code is a header code and you shouldn&#x27;t use it in claim submissions.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating the specific industry code list. Can be set to <code>ABK</code> - International Classification of Diseases Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) Principal Diagnosis or <code>ABF</code> - International Classification of Diseases Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) Diagnosis, <code>TQ</code> Systemized Nomenclature of Dentistry (SNODENT).</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>ABK</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>ABF</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>TQ</code></div></div></section></div></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card orthodontic treatment. Required when the claim contains services related to treatment for orthodontic purposes. You <strong>must</strong> include one of these properties in this object: <code>monthsCount</code>, <code>monthsRemaining</code>, or <code>treatmentIndicator</code>.</p><details authorized the plan to remit payment directly to the provider. Can be set to <code>N</code> - No (Payment should go to the patient), <code>Y</code> - Yes (Payment should go directly to the provider), or <code>W</code> - Not Applicable.</p><div><section pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A code identifying the type of claim. For example <code>DS</code> - Disability. Use <code>OF</code> when submitting Medicare Part D claims. Use <code>ZZ</code> when you don&#x27;t know the type of insurance. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/claims-code-lists#claim-filing-indicator-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Claims code lists</a> for a complete list.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Use this object to report prior payers&#x27; claim level adjustments that cause the amount paid to differ from the amount originally charged. Codes and associated amounts must come from either paper remittance advice or 835s (Electronic Remittance Advice) received on the claim. When the information originates from a paper remittance advice that does not use the standard Claim Adjustment Reason Codes, you must convert them to standard Claim Adjustment Reason Codes.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" is <code>true</code>. Required when Required when the claim is being sent in the payer-to-payer COB model AND the destination payer is secondary to this payer AND this payer has re-adjudicated the rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code identifying the type of identifier. Can be set to <code>II</code> - Standard Unique Health Identifier for each individual in the United States or <code>MI</code> - Member Identification Number. The code <code>MI</code> should be the subscriber&#x27;s identification number as assigned by the payer, such as their subscriber ID. You should also use <code>MI</code> in claims submitted to the Indian Health Service/Contract Health Services (IHS/CHS) Fiscal Intermediary for the purpose of reporting the Tribe Residency Code (Tribe County State). For IHS/CHS claims, you should also put the SSN in the <code>otherInsuredAdditionalIdentifier</code> property.)</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>II</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>MI</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b Use the designated <code>otherInsuredNameSuffix</code> property instead.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The total amount in dollars the payer has paid on this claim. It is acceptable to set this to <code>0</code> (Zero). This is required when you include the <code>payToPlan</code> object, and you should set it to the amount the Medicaid agency actually paid.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code identifying the payer&#x27;s level of responsibility for paying this claim. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/claims-code-lists#payment-responsibility-sequence-number-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Claims code lists</a> for a complete list.</p>
<ul>
<li>Either this property or <code>subscriber.paymentResponsibilityLevelCode</code> must be set to <code>P</code> to indicate the primary insurance payer. Stedi rejects claims - including secondary and tertiary claims - that don&#x27;t include information for the primary payer.</li>
<li>You may need to use other codes if the patient has multiple insurance policies. For example, if a patient is covered by both Medicare and an employer-sponsored commercial plan, you could bill the commercial payer first as <code>P</code> and then bill the Medicare payer second as <code>S</code>.</li>
</ul><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">Expand</button></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>A</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>B</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>C</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>D</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>E</code></div></div></div><div class="p-3 border-t"><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">View <!-- -->7<!-- --> more item<!-- -->s</button></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating whether the provider has on file a signed statement by the patient authorizing the release of medical data to other organizations. Can be set to <code>I</code> - Informed Consent to Release Medical Information or <code>Y</code> - Yes. Code <code>I</code> is required when the provider has not collected a signature AND state or federal laws do not require a signature be collected. Code <code>Y</code> is required when the provider has collected a signature OR when state or federal laws require a signature be collected.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>I</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>Y</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b class="font-mono">^\d+(\.\d{1,2})?$</code></span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The total amount in dollars the patient or their representatives have paid on this claim. For example, <code>20.50</code>. This includes any co-payments, co-insurance, or other amounts already collected from the patient.</p>
<p>If the patient has not paid anything, you should omit this property entirely - <strong>don&#x27;t</strong> set it to <code>0</code>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Length:<!-- --> <!-- -->1 - 20</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>An identifier you assign to the claim. We <strong>strongly recommend</strong> submitting a unique value for this property so you can use it to correlate this claim with responses, such as the 277CA claim acknowledgment and the 835 ERA.</p>
<ul>
<li>Use random strings. The identifier should be more complex than a simple sequential number and should be hard to guess. Formats with patient initials or the date of service in them can create duplicates. We recommend using <a href="https://www.npmjs.com/package/nanoid" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">nanoid</a> or a similar library to generate unique IDs.</li>
<li>Keep it to 17 characters max. Some payers cut off values longer than 17 characters in 277CAs and ERAs, which makes it hard to match them with the original claim.</li>
<li>Use alphanumeric characters only. Avoid special characters, as many payers don&#x27;t handle them properly.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none break-words">claimInformation.planParticipationCode</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The code indicating whether the provider accepts assignment in their relationship with the payer. Can be set to <code>A</code> - Assigned or <code>C</code> - Not Assigned. Code <code>A</code> is required when either the provider accepts assignment or has a participation agreement with the payer OR when the provider doesn&#x27;t accept assignment or have a participation agreement but is advising the payer to adjudicate this specific claim under participating provider benefits allowed in certain plans.</p>
<p>Note that this is <strong>not</strong> where you should indicate whether the patient has assigned benefits to the provider - you must indicate that in the <code>benefitsAssignmentCertificationIndicator</code> property.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>A</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>C</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property break-words">claimInformation.predeterminationOfBenefits</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" 23 12C23 15.866 19.866 19 16 19H8C4.13401 19 1 15.866 1 12C1 8.13401 4.13401 5 8 5ZM8 15C6.34315 15 5 13.6569 5 12C5 10.3431 6.34315 9 8 9C9.65685 9 11 10.3431 11 12C11 13.6569 9.65685 15 8 15Z"></path></svg>Boolean</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Required when the entire claim is being submitted as a predetermination of benefits. Predetermination of benefits claims are submitted in advance of services to get an estimate of what the patient&#x27;s health plan will pay.</p>
<ul>
<li>Can be set to <code>true</code> to indicate predetermination of dental benefits.</li>
<li>Not all dental payers support predetermination of benefits claims.</li>
<li>Some payers prohibit setting <code>claimInformation.claimDateInformation.serviceDate</code> for predetermination of benefits claims. Refer to your payer&#x27;s specific guidelines for more information.</li>
</ul></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Strings</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Items:<!-- --> <!-- -->1 - 2</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code identifying an accompanying cause of an illness, injury or an accident. Can be set to <code>AA</code> - Auto Accident, <code>EM</code> - Employment, or <code>OA</code> - Other Accident. You can include up to two codes in this array.</p><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md last:border-r-0"><code>AA</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>EM</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>OA</code></div></div></section></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Indicates whether the provider has on file a signed statement by the patient authorizing the release of medical data to other organizations. Can be set to <code>Y</code> - Yes, or <code>I</code> - Informed Consent to Release Medical Information for Conditions or Diagnoses Regulated by Federal Statues. Use <code>I</code> when the provider has not collected a signature AND state or federal laws do not require a signature be collected.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>I</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>Y</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b 5.44772 17.5523 5 17 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Required when the location for the service is different from the billing provider&#x27;s address. The purpose of this object is to identify specifically where the service was rendered. This can be healthcare facilities, such as surgical centers or reference labs, OR the patient&#x27;s address when services were rendered in their home.</p>
<ul>
<li>Only include this object when the service facility location is <strong>different</strong> from the billing provider&#x27;s address. If you include this object when the address is the same, Stedi omits all of the service facility location information from the claim submission, including the name and any identifiers.</li>
<li>For telehealth services, the service facility location is the provider&#x27;s address, even though the patient may have been in their home or elsewhere when receiving services.</li>
<li>Sometimes the billing provider is an actual physician group that is located at the same address as a hospital, but is in fact a separate entity. In this case, you can differentiate the service facility location by including the specific suite or building number of the physician group. This ensures that the service facility location is different from the billing provider&#x27;s address and is reported accurately.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The address of where services were rendered.</p>
<p>If the service facility location is in an area where there are no street addresses, enter a description of where the service was rendered. For example, &#x27;crossroad of State Road 34 and 45&#x27;.</p>
<p>For United States addresses, you <strong>must</strong> include the full nine-digit zip code with no separators, such as <code>100031502</code>. If you don&#x27;t know the full zip code, you can find it using the <a href="https://tools.usps.com/zip-code-lookup.htm" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">USPS ZIP Code Lookup</a> tool.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The phone number, formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB is the telephone number prefix, and CCCC is the telephone number. The phone number should only include the digits 0 to 9. Don&#x27;t include separators, such as dashes, and don&#x27;t include long distance access numbers, such as 1. For example, you would format the phone number 123-456-7890 as 1234567890.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about one or more services rendered to the patient.
- Each service line must be a unique service event as defined by the payer&#x27;s billing policies. This means that you can use the same procedure code on multiple service lines as long as they are distinct events.
- Some procedure codes are date-specific. In these cases, you may need to create a separate service line with that code for each applicable date of service, even if the episode of care extended over multiple days.
- Service lines can share the same dates of service if the patient received multiple services on the same day.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.claimInformation.serviceLines" id="body.claimInformation.serviceLines"><summary class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the assistant surgeon who rendered the care. Use this object when the rendering providers provided these services in the role of the assistant surgeon.</p>
<p>This should be an individual, not an organization, and you should supply at least the surgeon&#x27;s <code>lastName</code> and an identifier, which is typically the <code>npi</code>.</p><details 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Diagnosis code pointers in order of importance to this service line. These pointers are an index to the ICD-10-CM codes you included in the <code>claimInformation.healthCareCodeInformation</code> object array. The pointer values can be from 1 to 12 (integer numbers).</p>
<ul>
<li>You <strong>must</strong> set at least one pointer for the primary diagnosis. Then, you can add up to three additional pointers (up to four in total).</li>
<li>The number of pointers cannot exceed the number of diagnosis codes in the <code>claimInformation.healthCareCodeInformation</code> object array. For example, if you only supplied one diagnosis code, then the only valid pointer value is 1.</li>
<li>Don&#x27;t put ICD-10-CM codes here - they belong in <code>claimInformation.healthCareCodeInformation</code>.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" <li>You can include up to five codes per service line.</li>
<li>You should report individual tooth numbers in one or more <code>teethInformation</code> objects.</li>

class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating the placement status for the dental work. Can be set to <code>I</code> - Initial Placement or <code>R</code> - Replacement. When set to <code>R</code>, you must include either the <code>priorPlacementDate</code> or <code>estimatedPriorPlacementDate</code> properties within the <code>claimInformation.serviceLines.serviceLineDateInformation</code> object.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>I</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>R</code></div></div></section></div></div></div></div></details></div></div><div data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code identifying the general category of payment adjustment. Can be set to <code>CO</code> - Contractual Obligations, <code>CR</code> - Correction and Reversals, <code>OA</code> - Other Adjustments, <code>PI</code> - Payor Initiated Reductions, or `PR - Patient Responsibility.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>CO</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>CR</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>OA</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>PI</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>PR</code></div></div></div></section></div></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The number of paid units from the remittance advice. expressed as a decimal. When paid units are not present on the remittance advice, use the original billed units. The maximum length for this property is 8 digits excluding the decimal. When a decimal is used, the maximum number of digits allowed to the right of the decimal is three.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>A unique identifier for this service line within the claim. It appears in the 835 (ERA) response as <code>lineItemControlNumber</code>, allowing you to correlate ERAs to the specific service lines from the original claim. If you don&#x27;t set this property, Stedi uses a random ULID. Stedi returns service line identifiers in the <code>claimReference.serviceLines.lineItemControlNumber</code> object of the synchronous API response.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the provider who rendered the services. This can be a individual or a company (a laboratory or other facility). This is where you should enter the substitute provider&#x27;s (locum tenens physician) information, if applicable.</p>
<p>You should only include this object when the rendering provider information for this service line is different than the information listed in the <code>rendering</code> object for the entire claim.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" 18 18.5523 18 18V13.9C18 13.0142 18.5759 12.2628 19.3738 12C18.5759 11.7372 18 10.9858 18 10.1V6C18 5.44772 17.5523 5 17 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about where the services were rendered. This can be healthcare facilities, such as surgical centers or reference labs, OR the patient&#x27;s address when services were rendered in their home.</p>
<ul>
<li>Only include this object when the service facility location is <strong>different</strong> from the billing provider&#x27;s address. If you include this object when the address is the same, Stedi omits all of the service facility location information from the claim submission, including the name and any identifiers.</li>
<li>For telehealth services, the service facility location is the provider&#x27;s address, even though the patient may have been in their home or elsewhere when receiving services.</li>
<li>Sometimes the billing provider is an actual physician group that is located at the same address as a hospital, but is in fact a separate entity. In this case, you can differentiate the service facility location by including the specific suite or building number of the physician group. This ensures that the service facility location is different from the billing provider&#x27;s address and is reported accurately.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" This may apply to the following scenarios: initial impression or preparation for a crown or denture, reporting initial endontic treatment, or reporting the implant fixture placement. If you include this property, <strong>do not</strong> include the <code>serviceDate</code> property in this service line.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t provider who oversaw the rendering provider and the care reported in this service line. Include this object when the supervising provider is different than the one listed in the <code>supervising</code> object for the entire claim.</p>
<p>This should be an individual, not an organization, and you should supply at least the provider&#x27;s <code>lastName</code> and an identifier, which is typically the <code>npi</code>.</p><details pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>An <a href="https://www.ada.org/publications/cdt" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">American Dental Association CDT Code</a> for the procedures performed on a specific tooth. You can only use this object to report individual teeth. You can&#x27;t use it to report areas of the oral cavity, such as quadrants or sextants. Areas of the oral cavity are reported in the <code>claimInformation.serviceLines.dentalService.oralCavityDesignation</code> property. You can only include multiples of this object when <code>claimInformation.serviceLines.dentalService.procedureCount</code> is equal to 1. When applicable, you can include this object up to 32 times within a single service line.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card Can be set to <code>B</code> - Buccal, <code>D</code>- Distal, <code>F</code>- Facial, <code>I</code>- Incisal, <code>L</code> - Lingual, <code>M</code> - Mesial, or <code>O</code> Occlusal.</p><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating the Special Program under which the services rendered to the patient were performed. Used for Medicaid claims only. Can be set to <code>01</code> - Early &amp; Periodic Screening, Diagnosis and Treatment (EPSDT) or Child Assessment Program (CHAP), <code>02</code> - Physically Handicapped Children&#x27;s Program, <code>03</code> - Special Federal Funding, or <code>05</code> - Disability. Codes <code>02</code>, <code>03</code>, and <code>05</code> are used for Medicaid claims only.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Dependent who received the medical care associated with the claim.</p>
<ul>
<li>If the dependent has their own member ID for the health plan, you should include the dependent&#x27;s information in the <code>subscriber</code> object instead. To check whether a dependent has a member ID, submit an <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-eligibility" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Eligibility Check</a> to the payer. The payer returns the dependent&#x27;s member ID in the <code>dependents.memberId</code> property in the response, if present.</li>
<li>You must include <code>address</code> in this object when the patient is a dependent.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.dependent" 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The dependent&#x27;s address. Every claim must include address information in either the <code>subscriber</code> (when the patient is the subscriber) or <code>dependent</code> (when the patient is a dependent) object. You must include at least the <code>address1</code> and <code>city</code> properties in this object. The <code>state</code> and <code>postalCode</code> properties are also required for all United States and Canadian addresses.</p>
<ul>
<li>The address must be the patient&#x27;s correct address at the time of service. Don&#x27;t use placeholder values to complete unknown address information. Use of outdated or placeholder values could cause the payer to reject, deny, or delay the claim due to suspected fraud.</li>
<li>If you don&#x27;t know the patient&#x27;s address, you should first submit a <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/post-healthcare-eligibility" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Real-Time Eligibility Check</a> for the patient and then copy the patient&#x27;s address from either the <code>subscriber</code> or <code>dependent</code> object in the response.</li>
<li>If the patient doesn&#x27;t have a current address, you can populate the <code>address1</code> property with <code>UNKNOWN</code> and populate the city, state, and zip code with appropriate values based on your discretion. However, some payers may have explicit rules for how to handle this situation, so you should check the payer&#x27;s specific requirements before using this approach.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indiciating the patient&#x27;s gender. Can be set to <code>F</code> - Female, <code>M</code> - Male, or <code>U</code> - Unknown.</p>
<p>Some payers may reject the claim if the patient&#x27;s gender doesn&#x27;t match the gender they have recorded in their member records. If the gender isn&#x27;t known or the patient declines to answer, use <code>U</code> or perform an eligibility check to determine the gender according to the payer&#x27;s records.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>M</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>F</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>U</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Identifies the relationship of the patient to the subscriber. Can be set to <code>01</code> - Spouse, <code>19</code> - Child, <code>20</code> - Employee, <code>21</code> - Unknown, <code>39</code> - Organ Donor, <code>40</code> - Cadaver Donor, <code>53</code> - Life Partner, or <code>G8</code> - Other Relationship.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">Expand</button></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t billing provider for this claim.</p>
<p>This is also where you can provide the billing provider&#x27;s mailing address when it&#x27;s a PO Box, lockbox, or other non-physical address. The <code>billing.address</code> object must always contain a physical practice location where care is delivered.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 Can be set to <code>2U</code> - Payer Identification Number, <code>FY</code> - Claim Office Number, or <code>NF</code> - National Association of Insurance Commissioners. You should only set this to <code>2U</code> when you set the <code>primaryIdentifierTypeCode</code> to <code>XV</code>.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the provider who directed the patient to the rendering provider for care. For example, a primary care physician may refer patients to a specialist. Use when the referring provider applies to the entire claim, not just a specific service line.</p>
<p>This should be an individual, not an organization, and you should supply at least the provider&#x27;s <code>lastName</code> and an identifier, which is typically the <code>npi</code>.</p><details 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The phone number, formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB is the telephone number prefix, and CCCC is the telephone number. The phone number should only include the digits 0 to 9. Don&#x27;t include separators, such as dashes, and don&#x27;t include long distance access numbers, such as 1. For example, you would format the phone number 123-456-7890 as 1234567890.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x 13.0142 18.5759 12.2628 19.3738 12C18.5759 11.7372 18 10.9858 18 10.1V6C18 5.44772 17.5523 5 17 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the person or company (laboratory or other facility) who rendered the care. Use this object for all types of rendering providers including laboratories. When a substitute provider (locum tenens) was used, enter that provider&#x27;s information here.</p>
<ul>
<li>Use when the provider applies to the entire claim or to at least one service line. For example, if a claim had two service lines with two different rendering providers, you would include the provider for the first service line here and leave the <code>claimInformation.serviceLines.renderingProvider</code> object for that service line blank. Then, you would specify the second provider in the appropriate service line&#x27;s <code>claimInformation.serviceLines.renderingProvider</code> object.</li>
<li>You can omit this object when the rendering provider is the same as the billing provider. In that case, you would include the provider&#x27;s information in the <code>billing</code> object and leave this object blank.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.rendering" 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The phone number, formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB is the telephone number prefix, and CCCC is the telephone number. The phone number should only include the digits 0 to 9. Don&#x27;t include separators, such as dashes, and don&#x27;t include long distance access numbers, such as 1. For example, you would format the phone number 123-456-7890 as 1234567890.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x This can be either an individual or an organization, such as a doctor, hospital, or insurance company. You must submit at least <code>organizationName</code> or <code>lastName</code> properties and the <code>contactInformation</code> object. If you don&#x27;t supply the <code>submitterIdentification</code> property, Stedi uses the value from <code>billing.npi</code> in the request.</p><details AAA represents the area code, BBB is the telephone number prefix, and CCCC is the telephone number. The phone number should only include the digits 0 to 9. Don&#x27;t include separators, such as dashes, and don&#x27;t include long distance access numbers, such as 1. For example, you would format the phone number 123-456-7890 as 1234567890.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-transparent ring-1 ring-inset ring-neutral-300 dark:ring-neutral-700 text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 break-all">Length:<!-- --> <!-- -->2 - 80</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The submitter&#x27;s Electronic Transmitter Identification Number (ETIN), as assigned by the payer. For some payers, this may be the same as the submitter&#x27;s NPI or TIN, but it can also be another unique identifier. Payers can refer to this identifier as the Provider Number, Submitter ID, Submitter Identifier, Submitter Primary Number, Sender Code, Certified Contracted Provider ID, and other names.</p>
<p>If you don&#x27;t provide this property, Stedi uses the billing provider&#x27;s NPI from <code>billing.npi</code> property.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" 13.0142 18.5759 12.2628 19.3738 12C18.5759 11.7372 18 10.9858 18 10.1V6C18 5.44772 17.5523 5 17 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-950/50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 whitespace-nowrap break-all" data-component-part="field-required-pill">Required</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The person or entity who is the primary policyholder for the health plan <em>or</em> a dependent with their own member ID.</p>
<ul>
<li>When a dependent has a unique, payer-assigned member ID, treat them as the <code>subscriber</code> for the claim submission - include their information here and omit the <code>dependent</code> object from the request. Note that the subscriber can be an individual or a business entity. Stedi treats the subscriber as an individual when the request doesn&#x27;t contain a value for the <code>subscriber.organizationName</code> property.</li>
<li>You must set the <code>dateOfBirth</code> and <code>gender</code> properties when the subscriber is the patient. Stedi determines that the subscriber is the patient when the <code>dependent</code> object is not included in the request.</li>
<li>If either <code>dateOfBirth</code> or <code>gender</code> is set, you must include both properties. You can either include both properties or neither within a single request.</li>
<li>You must include <code>address</code> in this object when the patient is the subscriber. If the patient is a dependent, include address information in the <code>dependent</code> object instead.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.subscriber" mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg
